result.stdout:
usage: SigningServer.Client [options] [Source1 Source2 Source3 ...]
options: 
  --server Server, -s Server
      The URL to the Signing Server to use
      Config.json Key: "SigningServer": "value"
  --username [username], -u [username]
      The username to use for authentication and certificate selection on the server
      Config.json Key: "Username": "value"
  --password [password], -p [password]
      The password to use for authentication and certificate selection on the server
      Config.json Key: "Password": "value"
  --help, -h
      Print this help.
  --config File, -c File
      The path to the config.json to use (overwrites any previously provided settings)
  --hash-algorithm [algorithm], -h [algorithm]
      The Hash Algorithm to use for signing (empty for default)
      Config.json Key: "HashAlgorithm": "value"
  --rsa-signature-padding [algorithm]
      The RSA Signature Padding to use for signing (empty for default)
      Config.json Key: "RsaSignaturePaddingMode": "value"
  --overwrite-signatures, -os
      Enable overwriting of existing signatures
      Config.json Key: "OverwriteSignatures": true
  --keep-signatures, -ks
      Disable overwriting of existing signatures
      Config.json Key: "OverwriteSignatures": false
  --ignore-existing-signatures, -is
      Ignore existing signatures
      Config.json Key: "IgnoreExistingSignatures": true
  --fail-on-existing-signatures, -fs
      Whether to fail with existing signatures
      Config.json Key: "IgnoreExistingSignatures": false
  --ignore-unsupported-files, -iu
      Whether to ignore unsupported file formats
      Config.json Key: "IgnoreUnsupportedFiles": true
  --fail-on-unsupported-files, -fu
      Whether to fail when unsupported file formats
      Config.json Key: "IgnoreUnsupportedFiles": false
  --timeout Timeout, -t Timeout
      Configures the timeout in seconds before failing the signing operations.
      Config.json Key: "IgnoreUnsupportedFiles": 300
  --retries Retries, -re Retries
      The number of retries to attempt on potentially recoverable errors (e.g. timeouts).
      Config.json Key: "Retry": 3
  --parallel [NumberOfThreads], -pa [NumberOfThreads]
      The number of parallel signing operations the client should perform.
      Might be reduced based on the server configuration.
      Leave value empty (or null in config.json) for auto detection.
      Config.json Key: "Parallel": 4
  --sign-hash SignatureExtension
      Instead of uploading the file and signing it according to the known file
      format. The file will be hashed locally, and the hash is sent to the 
      server for signing. The signature will be written as raw bytes to the
      same paths as the input file with the extension changed to the provided file extension
  --load-certificate Format Path
      Instead of signing files a certificate file containing the public key will be downloaded
      to the specified path. Can be combined with other operations
      Format: 
        - "PemCertificate" - A PEM encoded file holding full X509 certificates (BEGIN/END CERTIFICATE)
        - "PemPublicKey" - A PEM encoded file holding Public Key Subject Infos  (BEGIN/END PUBLIC KEY)
        - "Pkcs12" - A Pkcs12 encoded file (aka. PFX).
  --load-certificate-chain Format
      Like --load-certificate but the whole certificate chian will be downloaded.

sources: 
   Can be any single file or a full directory (recursive) to sign.
   For directories only known supported files are considered.

exit codes: 
   1 - unexpected error
   2 - Specified source could not be found
   3 - Detected a file which is already signed and --fail-on-existing-signatures is set
   4 - Detected an unsupported file format and --fail-on-unsupported-files is active
   5 - Unauthorized, wrong username or password
   6 - Client configuration invalid
   7 - Communication error

commands.payload:

options:
